one_line_takeaway: Extending a prior 3-agent LLM prompt-injection pipeline with semantic-similarity caching (Nested-Learning-inspired MTM/LTM memory) and a 5th 'observability' metric, the authors report zero high-risk breaches and 84.4% secure outputs across 301 adversarial prompts, a 41.6% cut in LLM calls via a 0.87-cosine-similarity cache, and a counterintuitive result that the most observability-weighted TIVS-O configuration also scored best on security -- but all evaluation is done by an LLM judge (Claude Sonnet 4.5) whose own conflation of verbosity with vulnerability the authors flag as an unresolved limitation, and the new cache mechanism itself is not adversarially tested.
key_finding: On the 301-prompt adversarial corpus: 84.4% of final (Third Level) outputs were classified secure (ISR<0.2), 15.6% moderate risk, and 0% high-risk (ISR>=0.5) -- zero high-risk breaches. Per-layer mean ISR: Frontend 0.0284 (97.67% blocked), Second Level 0.0219 (98.01% blocked), Third Level 0.0762 (84.39% blocked, but 0 vulnerable prompts vs 7 and 6 at earlier layers). Final KPIs at Third Level: ISR=0.076, POF=0.059, PSR=0.986, CCS=0.933, OSR=0.596. On 100 benign prompts, false positive rate was 2.0% (2/100), mean benign ISR 0.003 vs 0.076 adversarial. Semantic caching (tau=0.87) produced an aggregate 41.6% hit rate (376 hits / 903 total calls across 301 prompts x 3 agents), reducing calls to 527; per-agent hit rates were Frontend 14.3%, Second Level 53.2%, Third Level 57.5%. Nested-Learning ablation: No Memory TIVS-O=-0.312 (ISR=0.089, 903 calls, 9.2s latency); MTM Only TIVS-O=-0.421 (25.6% cache hit); Full CMS TIVS-O=-0.521 (ISR=0.076, 527 calls, 3.8s latency, 41.6% hit) -- a claimed 67% TIVS-O improvement over No Memory. Across five TIVS-O weighting configurations, ExtremeObservability (weight skewed toward OSR) achieved the best (most negative) mean TIVS-O=-0.521 (std 0.088), beating SecurityFirst (-0.500) and Baseline (-0.476), the paper's central 'observability does not hurt security' claim. KPI evolution Frontend->Third Level: PSR +5.3% (0.933->0.986), OSR +29.1% (0.305->0.596), CCS -5.8% (0.992->0.933). Sustainability: for a hypothetical 100k-prompt workload, scaling the 41.6% call-reduction implies ~124,800 avoided LLM calls, translating (via three externally sourced Wh/prompt scenarios) to an estimated 30-3,619 kWh energy and 3.7-452 kg CO2e savings -- explicitly labeled order-of-magnitude, not measured.
core_contribution: Extends the authors' own prior four-metric TIVS multi-agent prompt-injection defense (Gosmar, Dahl & Gosmar, arXiv:2503.11517) with three own additions: (1) semantic similarity-based caching ('Continuum Memory Systems', CMS) attached to each of three pipeline agents, implementing a HOPE-inspired 'Nested Learning' two-tier memory (Medium-Term Memory with LRU eviction, Long-Term Memory with LFU-style consolidation) over an all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding space at cosine-similarity threshold tau=0.87; (2) a fourth, non-pipeline agent (Claude Sonnet 4.5) that scores every intermediate output on five KPIs (ISR, POF, PSR, CCS, and a newly introduced OSR -- Observability Score/Ratio) to compute a new composite metric TIVS-O across five weighting configurations (Baseline, ObservabilityAware, SecurityFirst, ResearchMode, ExtremeObservability); (3) an operational-sustainability analysis translating cache-driven reduction in LLM calls into order-of-magnitude energy/CO2e estimates. The three defended pipeline agents are Llama 2 7B (front-end), Llama 3.1 8B (guard-sanitizer and policy enforcer), run locally via Ollama; the KPI evaluator/judge is Claude Sonnet 4.5.
threat_model: Text-only prompt injection against an LLM-driven multi-agent pipeline: an attacker crafts an input that attempts to override/subvert the system's intended instructions (direct overrides, authority assertion, role-play/DAN, logical traps, multi-step escalation, obfuscation e.g. Base64, context injection via embedded documents, instruction confusion, simulated dialog history, goal hijacking). The paper explicitly scopes out multimodal injection, context-window-overflow attacks, and adversarial fine-tuning as future work, and does not model adversaries who specifically target or poison the semantic cache itself (e.g., crafting near-duplicate prompts to force stale/incorrect cache reuse) -- this is not analyzed as an attack surface despite the paper introducing that cache.
limitations: Paper's own stated limitations (Section 12): (1) the 301-prompt evaluation corpus is synthetic and finite, generated by an LLM and manually filtered, not drawn from real attacker logs; (2) the similarity threshold tau=0.87 was chosen empirically for this corpus/embedding model and the paper states it may not generalize to other domains, embedding models, or false-positive tolerances; (3) the fourth-agent LLM-judge (used for ISR/POF/PSR/CCS/OSR scoring) is itself a learned, potentially biased component -- the paper explicitly flags that it 'can conflate explanatory verbosity with vulnerability,' penalizing detailed forensic reasoning as a partial concession even when content stays compliant, and that scores are 'approximate signals rather than ground-truth labels'; (4) the general-purpose all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding is not fine-tuned for security and may conflate semantically distinct attack patterns (e.g., 'You are an admin' vs. 'Act as if you are an admin') or drift as attackers adopt new phrasing; (5) direct comparison with the authors' prior TIVS work is explicitly called out by the authors as not apples-to-apples (different metric formulation -- four vs five weighted terms -- and different corpus size, 500 vs 301 prompts), so the headline '67% vs 45.7% vulnerability reduction' comparison is qualitative, not a controlled ablation; (6) sustainability numbers are order-of-magnitude estimates anchored to third-party public energy-per-prompt disclosures, not direct power measurement of the authors' own Ollama deployment -- the paper explicitly defers 'direct power measurement' to future work; (7) no adversarial analysis of the cache itself (e.g., prompts engineered to force false cache hits or exploit tau=0.87) is conducted, despite caching being a new, potentially exploitable component introduced by this paper.

datasets_used: 301 synthetic injection-focused prompts spanning 10 attack families (Direct Override, Authority Assertion, Role-Play, Logical Trap, Multi-Step, Obfuscation, Context Injection, Instruction Confusion, Simulated Dialog, Goal Hijacking) -- LLM-generated, manually filtered
100 benign prompts (informational queries, translation requests, educational questions, task assistance) for false-positive testing
quantitative_results: Security classification: 254/301 (84.4%) secure ISR<0.2, 47/301 (15.6%) moderate risk, 0/301 (0.0%) high risk ISR>=0.5
False positive rate on 100 benign prompts: 2.0% (mean ISR 0.003 vs 0.076 adversarial mean)
Per-layer mean ISR / blocked rate / vulnerable count: Frontend 0.0284 / 97.67% / 7; Second Level 0.0219 / 98.01% / 6; Third Level 0.0762 / 84.39% / 0
Final Third-Level KPIs: ISR=0.076, POF=0.059, PSR=0.986, CCS=0.933, OSR=0.596
Semantic cache hit rates (tau=0.87): Frontend 43/301=14.3%, Second Level 160/301=53.2%, Third Level 173/301=57.5%, Total 376/903=41.6%

Nested Learning ablation TIVS-O: No Memory -0.312 (ISR 0.089, 903 LLM calls, 9.2s latency, 0% cache); MTM Only -0.421 (ISR 0.081, 672 calls, 4.8s, 25.6% cache); Full CMS -0.521 (ISR 0.076, 527 calls, 3.8s, 41.6% cache) -- reported as +67% TIVS-O improvement, -14.6% ISR, -59% latency, -42% LLM calls (Full vs None)

TIVS-O configuration comparison (Third Level, mean/std/strong<-0.6/weak>-0.3): ExtremeObservability -0.521/0.088/16(5.3%)/12(4.0%); ResearchMode -0.506/0.092/14(4.7%)/18(6.0%); SecurityFirst -0.500/0.085/13(4.3%)/15(5.0%); ObservabilityAware -0.491/0.090/11(3.7%)/21(7.0%); Baseline -0.476/0.095/9(3.0%)/25(8.3%)
Cross-layer ISR improvement counts: 31 prompts (10.3%) improved Frontend->Second; 23 prompts (7.6%) improved Second->Third; 35 prompts (11.6%) improved end-to-end Frontend->Third

Formal latency model: baseline Tbaseline=903x3s=2709s (~45.2 min); Tcached=527x3s+376x0.05s=1599.8s (~26.7 min); steady-state fully-cached per-prompt latency 150ms (3x50ms) vs 9s baseline = 60x speedup

Sustainability projection (100k-prompt workload, hypothetical, not measured): baseline 300k calls, 41.6% reduction => 124,800 avoided calls / 175,200 executed; estimated energy savings 30.0/52.4/3,619 kWh under Efficient/Typical/Heavy per-prompt energy scenarios (0.24/0.42/29 Wh); estimated CO2e savings 3.7/6.6/452 kg using a derived 0.125 g CO2e/Wh factor

Comparison to authors' own prior work (arXiv:2503.11517, 500-prompt corpus, four-metric TIVS): prior TIVS3=-0.0932 with 45.7% vulnerability reduction, vs current Full CMS TIVS-O=-0.521 with 67% reduction -- authors explicitly caveat this as not a controlled comparison (different metric formulation and corpus size)
Appendix example prompts: mean Frontend-to-Final ISR improvement of 0.44 (84% relative reduction), e.g. Direct Override prompt ISR 0.78 (Frontend) -> 0.02 (Final)
baselines_compared: No Memory (memoryless multi-agent pipeline, same 3 agents, no CMS) -- internal ablation baseline
MTM Only (medium-term cache without long-term consolidation) -- internal ablation
Authors' own prior four-metric TIVS system (Gosmar, Dahl & Gosmar 2025, arXiv:2503.11517), 500-prompt corpus -- cited as prior work, not re-run in this paper (explicitly non-controlled comparison)

No external third-party prompt-injection defense system (e.g., PromptShield, Signed-Prompt) is empirically run or benchmarked in this paper; those are discussed only in Related Work as cited prior art, not as experimental baselines
